paper_title: Impact of ophthalmic clinical service use in mitigating myopia onset and progression in preschool children: a retrospective cohort study

critical_errors.description: Tardily group size reported as 1,012 instead of correct 1,215
Timely group size reported as 315 instead of correct 112
High-risk cohort N reported as 4,443 instead of correct 2,437
SE progression values for 'all children' attributed to 'high-risk group'
22 references appear AI-generated rather than extracted from original

strengths: Well-structured Introduction following classic funnel pattern from broad to specific
Strong prose quality and readability across all sections
Good logical flow and narrative coherence in Results presentation
Appropriate use of statistical terminology and effect estimate reporting
Comprehensive literature context added to Introduction and Discussion
weaknesses: Multiple critical numerical errors in group sizes propagated throughout
Missing methodological detail (instruments, protocols, formulas, software)
Under-developed Discussion missing key thematic sections and policy implications
SE progression data conflated between analytic subgroups
Fabricated reference list rather than extracted from original
